content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 6, 1990

Judy Conover
Senator Robert Presley
6355 Riverside Avenue
Riverside, CA 92506

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-218

Dear Ms. Conover:

You have requested advice on behalf of Senator Robert Presley
regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

FACTS

Friends of Robert Presley issued a check to Telequery, Inc.
in the amount of $19,200 as a "start-up" fee for a telemarketing
solicitation effort on behalf of the committee. As a result of
the telemarketing effort, the $19,200 will be remitted in full
from contributions received. Contributions in excess of the
$19,200 will be paid to the committee after a $30.00 per hour
service charge has been deducted.

QUESTION

How should the "start-up" money be reported?

CONCLUSION

The $19,200 paid to Telequery, Inc. should be reported as an
expenditure for telemarketing services on Schedule E (Payments and
Contributions (Other Than Loans) Made) of the Form 490.


1/ Government Code Section 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2 Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Judy Conover
Page 2

ANALYSIS

The Act's campaign disclosure provisions (Government Code
Sections 84100-84400) require that candidates disclose all
contributions received and expenditures made. Government Code
Section 82025 defines expenditure as:

....a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, payment of a loan by
a third party, or an enforceable promise to make a payment,
unless it is clear from the surrounding circumstances that it
is not made for political purposes...

Regulation 18225 (copy enclosed) further defines the term
"expenditure." Section 18225(a) states:

An expenditure is any monetary or non-monetary payment made
for political purposes. A payment is made for political
purposes if it is:...

(2) Made by:

(A) A candidate, unless it is clear from surrounding
circumstances that the payment was made for
personal purposes unrelated to his or her
candidacy or status as an officeholder....

According to our conversation, the money paid to Telequery,
Inc. was a start-up fee for fundraising efforts. Although the
money will be reimbursed to the campaign committee, the initial
start up money is for campaign services. Such expenditures are
reported on Schedule E of the Form 490.

During our conversation, you also asked how contributions
received through the telemarketing agency should be reported.
However, we do not have adequate information concerning the
procedure Telequery, Inc. is using to reimburse the campaign
committee for the initial payment of $19,200 and the handling of
contributions as a result of the fundraising efforts. Please
provide in writing detailed information so we can provide the
correct advice and reporting procedures.

If you have any questions regarding this matter, please feel
free to call me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

[illegible]

By: Linda Moureaux
Political Reform Consultant
